workflow.name: Client Identity Verification — FINTRAC Compliance
workflow.description: This procedure establishes the steps required to verify the identity of new clients opening accounts with Wealthsimple, in compliance with the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act (PCMLTFA) and FINTRAC guidelines.

steps.title: Receive New Client Application
steps.description: When a new client submits an account opening application through the Wealthsimple app or website, the system automatically captures the client's information and stores the application in the onboarding queue.

steps.title: Screen Against Sanctions and PEP Lists
steps.description: The system automatically screens the client against various sanctions and PEP lists, logging the results with relevant details.

steps.title: Assess Client Risk Level
steps.description: Based on the screening results and client profile, the system assigns an initial risk rating and determines if the client is flagged as high risk.

steps.title: Perform Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD)
steps.description: For high-risk clients, a compliance officer reviews the screening results, obtains additional information, and documents the rationale for proceeding or declining the application.

steps.title: Verify Client Identity — Select Method
steps.description: The client's identity must be verified using one of the FINTRAC-approved methods based on whether the client is onboarding digitally or in-person.

steps.title: Government-Issued Photo Identification Method
steps.description: The client uploads a valid government-issued photo ID, which is verified by the system and reviewed by a compliance officer.

steps.title: Credit File Method
steps.description: The system verifies the client's identity by matching information against Canadian credit bureau records.

steps.title: Dual-Process Method
steps.description: This method uses two independent sources from different categories to verify the client's identity.

steps.title: Create Compliance Record and Open Account
steps.description: Upon successful identity verification, a verification record is created, stored, and the client's account is opened.

steps.title: Escalate to Senior Compliance Officer
steps.description: When identity verification fails, the case is escalated to the Senior Compliance Officer for further review and decision-making.

steps.title: End of Procedure
steps.description: The procedure concludes here, with all necessary actions taken based on the verification outcome.

receive_new_client_application.description: When a new client submits an account opening application through the Wealthsimple app or website, the system automatically captures the client's information and stores the application in the onboarding queue.
receive_new_client_application.prompt_template:
Analyze the client data and execute: Receive New Client Application.
Consider all relevant compliance requirements.
Return a JSON object with result, data_gathered, flags, and status fields.

assess_client_risk_level.prompt_template:
Evaluate the condition: Assess Client Risk Level.
Based on previous step results and client data, determine the appropriate branch.
Options: Yes, No

perform_enhanced_due_diligence.description: For high-risk clients, a compliance officer reviews the screening results, obtains additional information, and documents the rationale for proceeding or declining the application.

Evaluate the condition: Verify Client Identity — Select Method.
Based on previous step results and client data, determine the appropriate branch.
Options: Government Photo ID, Credit File, Dual Process

credit_file_method.description: The system verifies the client's identity by matching information against Canadian credit bureau records.
credit_file_method.prompt_template:
Analyze the client data and execute: Credit File Method.
Consider all relevant compliance requirements.
Return a JSON object with result, data_gathered, flags, and status fields.

dual_process_method.description: This method uses two independent sources from different categories to verify the client's identity.

create_compliance_record_and_open_accoun.description: Upon successful identity verification, a verification record is created, stored, and the client's account is opened.
create_compliance_record_and_open_accoun.prompt_template:
Analyze the client data and execute: Create Compliance Record and Open Account.
Consider all relevant compliance requirements.
Return a JSON object with result, data_gathered, flags, and status fields.

escalate_to_senior_compliance_officer.description: When identity verification fails, the case is escalated to the Senior Compliance Officer for further review and decision-making.

end.description: The procedure concludes here, with all necessary actions taken based on the verification outcome.
